features.name: Email/Password Login
features.description: User logs in with email and password using bcrypt comparison against stored hash, returns JWT token valid for 7 days

issues: Returns 'Invalid credentials' for both non-existent user and wrong password (intentional security practice)
OAuth-only user detection checks passwordHash and googleId but not all oauth methods
No login attempt tracking or account lockout mechanism

gaps: failed login attempts not tracked
no brute force detection
no device/location tracking

businessValue.rationale: Daily access path for existing users; essential for retention and engagement
businessValue.userImpact: Returning users with email/password accounts

features.name: Email/Password Signup
features.description: User creates account with email and password, password is bcrypt-hashed with 12 rounds, verification email sent with secure token

issues: generateUsername logic duplicated in oauth.ts
Email verification token generation appears in both auth.ts and resend-verification
Rate limiting applied correctly but CAPTCHA validation could return early
Global signup throttle uses single shared bucket key

gaps: no signup failure rate tracking
no email delivery confirmation
no captcha failure metrics

businessValue.rationale: Direct user acquisition mechanism; every new user enters here
businessValue.userImpact: All new users creating accounts with email/password method

features.name: Email Verification
features.description: Signup generates secure emailVerificationToken, user clicks link to GET /verify-email which marks emailVerified=true; resend-verification endpoint for authenticated users; cleanup job removes stale tokens 24h old

issues: Cleanup interval in oauth.ts (60 min) removes tokens but auth.ts sends verification email on signup
No rate limiting on resend-verification endpoint
Double-click on verification link is handled but not logged distinctly
Token generation in signup and resend duplicated (should extract helper)
Redirect URL constructed with FRONTEND_URL hardcoded in route

existingMetrics: verification attempt logging with outcome field
gaps: no verification rate tracking
no email delivery confirmation
no resend request frequency

businessValue.rationale: Prevents spam signups and validates user email; less critical for OAuth users who are pre-verified
businessValue.userImpact: Email/password signup users

features.name: GitHub Verification
features.description: Authenticated user verifies GitHub account; exchanges OAuth code for access token, fetches user profile (login, name, avatar), prevents URL spoofing by constructing canonical URL, marks githubVerified=true

issues: GitHub and LinkedIn verification flows are nearly identical - significant duplication
Error handling for GitHub token exchange could distinguish between 401/403/500
No validation that githubId is numeric before converting to string
User profile fetch doesn't check for archived/deleted accounts
avatarUrl is fetched but not stored (unlike LinkedIn)

gaps: no verification completion rate
no GitHub API error tracking
no profile fetch latency

businessValue.rationale: Developer credibility signal; niche but valuable for technical hiring
businessValue.userImpact: Developer users wanting to display GitHub verification

compositeGoal.name: GitHub Verification Rate
compositeGoal.formula: github verified users / developer profile users

features.name: Google OAuth
features.description: Login/signup via Google using OAuth2 flow with state token validation; supports account linking by email match; new users marked emailVerified=true; stores oauthPhotoUrl

issues: Profile caching (pendingOAuthProfiles Map) not cleared on state expiry in all paths
Terms acceptance flow caches profile in memory but doesn't persist across server restart
LinkedIn and Google callback logic very similar - could be consolidated
ID token missing check could be more explicit about payload validation

gaps: no Google token exchange failure rate
no consent screen abandonment tracking
no account link conflict detection events

businessValue.rationale: Major signup flow for users with Google accounts; reduces friction
businessValue.userImpact: Users who prefer single sign-on via Google

compositeGoal.name: Google OAuth Completion Rate
compositeGoal.formula: successful google logins / google consent screen visits

features.name: LinkedIn OAuth
features.description: Login/signup via LinkedIn OIDC with code exchange; fetches userinfo and headline; supports account linking by email; new users marked emailVerified=true; stores linkedinHeadline

issues: Headline fetch is non-fatal but logged as warning - could be more explicit about optional fields
LinkedIn profile caching (pendingLinkedInProfiles) not cleared on timeout
Profile API endpoint hardcoded - brittle for API changes
No validation that headline string is reasonable length

gaps: no headline fetch success rate
no LinkedIn API failure tracking
no profile enrichment metrics

businessValue.rationale: Professional network tie-in; useful for credibility signaling but not core signup path
businessValue.userImpact: Professional users with LinkedIn accounts; those wanting credibility signals

features.name: LinkedIn Verification
features.description: Authenticated user can verify LinkedIn account via OAuth callback; fetches LinkedIn ID and profile; prevents URL spoofing by always overwriting linkedinUrl to canonical form; marks linkedinVerified=true

issues: URL spoofing prevention comment is good but implementation only works if LinkedIn ID fetch succeeds
linkedinHeadline update is non-fatal but could fail silently if profile was already verified
No check that new LinkedIn ID doesn't belong to different user before update
Verification flow very similar to GitHub verification - code duplication

gaps: no verification completion rate
no verification conflict tracking
no re-verification attempts

businessValue.rationale: Trust signal for professional users; part of credibility system
businessValue.userImpact: Users wanting to display LinkedIn verification badge

features.name: Password Reset
features.description: Forgot-password endpoint returns same message for user/non-user (prevents enumeration), generates secure token valid 1 hour, invalidates prior tokens; reset endpoint validates token expiry and status, updates password in transaction

issues: Forgot-password returns success for OAuth-only users but they can't reset (should have clearer messaging)
verify-reset-token endpoint doesn't check usedAt field initially but does check expiry
No rate limiting on forgot-password per email (only per IP via authRateLimiter)
Password reset transaction is good but could fail silently if human lookup fails

gaps: no password reset completion tracking
no token expiry metrics
no reset success rate

businessValue.rationale: Critical recovery path for locked-out users; impacts retention and support cost
businessValue.userImpact: Users who forgot password or locked out of email

features.name: Session Management
features.description: JWT tokens issued for 7 days with userId in payload; logout-all invalidates all tokens by setting tokenInvalidatedAt timestamp; auth middleware checks token expiry and invalidation time; unsubscribe endpoint uses JWT with action='unsubscribe'

issues: JWT_SECRET must be set in env - no validation that it exists
Unsubscribe token action checking is good but could be enum-based
No refresh token mechanism (tokens are long-lived)
logout-all only sets timestamp but doesn't revoke stored sessions/device tokens
Test waits 50ms for tokenInvalidatedAt to differ - flaky timestamp comparison

gaps: no token expiry distribution tracking
no logout-all usage metrics
no session duration analytics

businessValue.rationale: Security foundational; prevents unauthorized access but not directly revenue-impacting
businessValue.userImpact: All authenticated users

features.name: WhatsApp OTP Login
features.description: Two-step OTP login/signup via WhatsApp: send 6-digit code via Twilio, verify within 5 minutes (3 attempts max); creates account with phone as identity for new users

issues: OTP generation using crypto.randomInt is not constant-time
Placeholder email format 'wa_{phone}@whatsapp.hp.internal' visible in code
Attempt limit (4) buried in code, should be constant
Email verification not required for WhatsApp signups (security gap)
No test coverage for the WhatsApp flow despite it being signup path
Referrer validation duplicated from email signup (generateUsername import)

gaps: no OTP delivery success tracking
no attempt count metrics
no phone verification confidence scoring

businessValue.rationale: Mobile-first signup for emerging markets; reduces friction for unbanked users
businessValue.userImpact: Mobile users in markets with WhatsApp penetration; users without email

features.name: Push Notifications
features.description: Web Push API integration using VAPID keys for cross-browser push notification subscriptions. Per-user subscription cap (max 10 devices), SSRF protection on subscription endpoints, optional rate limiting.

issues: Zod schema validation could be extracted to shared module
No subscription health check mechanism (endpoints may go stale)
Missing monitoring for failed push delivery attempts
No option to gracefully remove failed subscriptions

gaps: No tracking of subscription success vs. failures
Missing metrics on stale endpoint detection
No monitoring of per-user device growth
Missing push delivery success rates

businessValue.rationale: Provides additional notification channel for time-sensitive alerts (job offers, messages) without relying on email or SMS. Desktop/mobile awareness.
businessValue.userImpact: Freelancers get instant desktop notifications for new job offers, increasing response time and acceptance rates.

compositeGoal.name: Push Notification Reliability
compositeGoal.target: >95% subscription success, >97% delivery rate, <5% stale endpoints

features.name: Social Posting Queue
features.description: Admin-managed queue of social media posting tasks (Facebook, YouTube, Podcast, Blog comments/reviews). Task types: fb_post, yt_comment, yt_reply, blog_comment, podcast_email, podcast_review, podcast_comment, podcast_reply. Ad copy repository (language/ad-number keyed) with task associations. Staff productivity tracking with daily breakdown.

issues: Large groups endpoint with complex filtering (status, language, country, adId, taskType, campaign)
Staff stats endpoint uses raw SQL with parameterization but complex conditional logic
Priority field as plain integer (no enum for priority levels)
Ad creation allows duplicate adNumber+language (handled by unique constraint, not validation)
Extension version/download endpoints serve files directly from filesystem (no abstraction)

existingMetrics: Staff completion counts with daily breakdown
gaps: No tracking of task cycle time (created → completed)
Missing metrics on task rejection reasons
No monitoring of ad copy performance (which ads get posted most)
Missing alerts for stale PENDING tasks
No tracking of language/country distribution of tasks

businessValue.rationale: Enables staff to coordinate social media engagement campaigns across platforms. Improves brand visibility and community engagement.
businessValue.userImpact: Staff can manage multi-platform posting tasks with priority and language targeting. Extension allows easy in-app task creation.

compositeGoal.name: Posting Queue Efficiency
compositeGoal.target: >50 tasks/week, >4.2/5.0 quality, >10 tasks/staff/day, >2.5% engagement rate

features.name: Telegram Bot Integration
features.description: Allows users to link their Telegram accounts for receiving notifications and initiating account linking via deep links. Webhook-based message handling with verification code flow, supports multi-language responses via i18n.

issues: In-memory pendingCodes store (no distributed cache for multi-instance deployments)
No rate limiting on webhook endpoint itself
Verification code stored in circular buffer without proper persistence
Error responses logged but not monitored for failures

existingMetrics: Debug endpoint for recent webhook events (circular buffer)
Logging for successful linkages and errors
gaps: No metrics on failed link attempts or verification timeouts
No monitoring of webhook signature verification failures
Missing SLA tracking for bot availability

businessValue.rationale: Enables push notifications for job offers and critical updates. Direct communication channel reduces email fatigue and increases engagement rates.
businessValue.userImpact: Freelancers receive real-time job notifications via familiar messaging app. Critical for reducing application latency.

compositeGoal.name: Telegram Integration Reliability
compositeGoal.target: >99% uptime, >95% link success, >98% delivery within 5s

features.name: User Feedback
features.description: Anonymous and authenticated feedback submission (bug reports, feature requests, general feedback) with rate limiting, CAPTCHA for anonymous submissions, auto-captured context (browser, OS, URL, viewport, screenshot), admin review dashboard with filtering by type/status, email notifications via Resend.

issues: Screenshot data base64 limited to 2MB (but stored in DB — should be externalized)
Admin list endpoint duplicated with ai endpoint (separate auth methods but same logic)
CAPTCHA verification depends on external service with no offline fallback
Email notification is fire-and-forget with minimal retry
Contact info prepending logic is string-based, brittle

existingMetrics: Logging of feedback submissions with type and user ID
gaps: No metrics on CAPTCHA verification success/failure rates
Missing tracking of feedback processing time (submission to admin review)
No monitoring of email notification delivery failures
Missing sentiment analysis tracking
No trend analysis on feedback topics

businessValue.rationale: Provides low-friction channel for users to report bugs, suggest features, and provide feedback. Critical input for product roadmap and bug detection. Email notifications ensure timely admin response.
businessValue.userImpact: Users can submit feedback without leaving the app. Urgent bugs (high severity) can be escalated to admin immediately.

compositeGoal.name: Feedback System Quality
compositeGoal.target: >500 feedback items/month, <2h response time, >70% bugs resolved, >95% valid submissions

features.name: WhatsApp Messaging
features.description: Twilio-based WhatsApp integration supporting account linking via dynamic link codes (HP-XXXX-XX format), inbound message routing to active jobs with disambiguation, pending message queues for 24h window enforcement, and conversational signup flow.

issues: Large webhook handler with 200+ lines covering 6 different flows (link, signup, disambiguation, routing, pending flush, keyword handling)
Status priority array defined locally; no reuse across modules
Duplicate link code validation logic in multiple places (handleLinkCode, inbound signup, keyword handler)
No timeout/retry logic for sendWhatsAppMessage calls
Magic number TL constants (48h, 24h, 10min, 30min) scattered throughout
No idempotency checks for duplicate inbound messages

existingMetrics: Logging of successful links and inbound signup completions
gaps: No tracking of link code expiration vs. usage rates
Missing metrics on disambiguation timeouts and re-prompts
No monitoring of pending message queue backlog or flush failures
Missing Twilio signature verification failure tracking
No SLA tracking for inbound message routing latency

businessValue.rationale: Two-way messaging channel for job interactions. Enables self-serve signup with no login friction. Job message routing is primary communication path for active work.
businessValue.userImpact: Freelancers can join and respond to jobs via WhatsApp without navigating website. Job publishers can contact workers synchronously.

compositeGoal.name: WhatsApp Messaging Reliability
compositeGoal.target: >92% link success, >8% signup conversion, >99% routing accuracy, >99.9% Twilio delivery

features.name: Blog (Public)
features.description: Public-facing blog read endpoint serving published BLOG platform ContentItems. Paginated list with featured articles first, individual post retrieval by slug, no auth required.

issues: No caching headers (ETags, Cache-Control) on post detail endpoint
No pagination security (limit capped at 50, good, but no offset validation)
Slug retrieval uses findFirst with no uniqueness constraint verification

gaps: No tracking of blog view counts or engagement metrics
Missing 404 error tracking for invalid slugs
No monitoring of page load time
Missing analytics on featured article performance

businessValue.rationale: Public-facing content for SEO, thought leadership, and user education. Drives organic traffic and establishes credibility.
businessValue.userImpact: Users can discover and read articles about HumanPages, AI agents, and freelancing without login.

compositeGoal.name: Blog Performance
compositeGoal.target: <2s load, >3% CTR, top 10 for target keywords, >5% share rate

features.name: Content Management
features.description: Admin content item lifecycle: ingest from blog engine (Twitter, LinkedIn, Blog), status workflow (DRAFT → REVIEW → APPROVED → PUBLISHED → REJECTED), cross-posting to Dev.to and Hashnode, image upload/generation, rejection feedback collection for pipeline learning.

issues: Large ingest endpoint handles 50 items in loop without transaction/batch
Slug collision handling uses Date.now() (non-deterministic, race condition possible)
Publish logic returns manualInstructions when API not configured (fallback but not ideal)
Cross-posting stores errors as JSON but no deduplication of repeated failures
Image generation tied to specific library (@resvg) — no abstraction
Error handling in feedback endpoint uses raw date filtering without validation

gaps: No metrics on ingest processing time or rejection rate
Missing tracking of cross-post failures vs. successes
No monitoring of publish API failures vs. manual fallback usage
Missing metrics on image generation cost/failures
No trend analysis on content pipeline velocity

businessValue.rationale: Core content amplification engine. Manages distribution across Twitter, LinkedIn, Dev.to, Hashnode, and internal blog. Rejection feedback improves AI content generation quality.
businessValue.userImpact: Blog content automatically syndicated across multiple platforms. Staff can approve/reject/edit before publishing.

compositeGoal.name: Content Pipeline Efficiency
compositeGoal.target: <4h end-to-end, >4.5/5.0 quality, >98% publish success, >80% crossposted to 2+ platforms

features.name: OG Image Generation
features.description: SVG-based Open Graph image generation for link previews. Template-based SVG generation (default, blog, careers, listings, profiles, platform connections). PNG rendering via resvg library. In-memory caching for static images. No auth required (except listing images for active listings).

issues: SVG generation functions have some code duplication (color schemes, gradients)
Platform list for connect/:platform is hardcoded (should be config)
No validation that blog/:slug actually exists (404 check could be more explicit)
Cache maps could have size limits (no eviction for rarely accessed platforms)

gaps: No tracking of 404 errors on non-existent resources
Missing metrics on cache hit rates
No monitoring of SVG → PNG rendering time
Missing alerts for expired listing images still being requested

businessValue.rationale: Improves social sharing appearance for job listings and profiles. Better social previews → higher click-through rates on shared links.
businessValue.userImpact: Job listings and profiles look professional when shared on Twitter/LinkedIn, increasing applications and discovery.

compositeGoal.name: OG Image Performance
compositeGoal.target: <200ms render time, >80% cache hit, 5/5 visual quality, >99.9% availability

features.name: Photo Concepts
features.description: Photo concept generation and rendering pipeline. Concepts stored in batch JSON with post type (meme_classic, meme_caption, quote_card, stat_card, etc.) and target platforms. Status tracking (new → approved → rendered). Assessment scoring via Python-based quality evaluator. Batch generation, individual rendering, output file serving.

issues: Very large file (780+ lines) mixing JSON file I/O, Python subprocess spawning, and HTTP endpoints
JSON sanitization function needed (control character filtering) — fragile parsing
Status sync logic duplicated (syncStatus called in multiple places without caching)
Python subprocess calls use spawn with detached:true (no result tracking or error propagation)
Assessment scoring requires full Python environment (no fallback if unavailable)
File serving endpoints have manual path construction (potential for traversal, though guarded)
No transaction semantics for moving concept from batch → queue → output

existingMetrics: Assessment score caching in status file
gaps: No metrics on batch generation progress/failures
Missing tracking of assessment accuracy vs. human review
No monitoring of render time by post type
Missing alerts for failed renders stuck in 'new' state
No tracking of post type performance (which types convert best)

businessValue.rationale: Generates social media content (memes, quotes, stats cards) at scale. Leverages AI to create platform-specific assets and optimize for engagement.
businessValue.userImpact: Admins can generate content batches, assess quality, and render to multiple platforms in one workflow.

compositeGoal.name: Photo Content Pipeline Quality
compositeGoal.target: >100 concepts/batch, >88% score accuracy, >92% render success, >3% engagement

features.name: Video Batches
features.description: Batch concept approval system for video generation pipelines. Lists video concepts from batch manifests (date-organized), tracks approval status (nano, draft, final tiers), provides script editing and concept detail viewing, concept gallery with approval filtering, image serving (local and R2-backed).

issues: Approved entries stored in JSON file (approved.json) with manual read/write (race condition possible in multi-instance)
Batch manifest loading fails silently (try/catch returns null, no error logging)
Date validation regex fragile (YYYY-MM-DD only, no leap year checks)
Concept number padding relies on ad-hoc string.padStart (should be constant)
Image serving has path traversal protection but filesystem assumption (no abstraction for cloud-native)
Script upload endpoint puts directly to filesystem (should queue for async processing)

gaps: No tracking of approval queue depth
Missing metrics on batch processing time
No monitoring of concept success/failure rates
Missing alerts for concepts with missing scripts
No tracking of image generation costs

businessValue.rationale: Manages video concept review and approval workflow for batch generation pipelines. Bottleneck for content production velocity.
businessValue.userImpact: Admins can visually review and approve video concepts before production rendering, reducing wasted compute on low-quality outputs.

compositeGoal.name: Video Batch Throughput
compositeGoal.target: <4h batch cycle, >85% approval rate, >4.2/5.0 quality score, <$200/batch

features.name: Video Concepts
features.description: Video concept authoring and management system. Markdown-based concept files with YAML frontmatter (title, duration, style). Approval workflow (new → approved → in_production/rendered). Job queue for preview (PREVIEW) and production (PRODUCE) runs. Cost estimation by tier based on image/video providers and pricing. Script editing, image regeneration for failed scenes, cost breakdown.

issues: Very large file (950+ lines) mixing multiple concerns: markdown parsing, job queue, cost estimation, image regeneration
Pricing constants defined inline (should be externalized config)
Script regeneration uses execFile with detached process (fire-and-forget, no result tracking)
Cost estimation duplicated across endpoints (no shared function)
In-memory regenInProgress Set (no distributed coordination for multi-instance)
Frontmatter parsing is manual YAML regex (should use YAML library)
No atomic operations on file writes (backup logic is try/catch, not robust)

gaps: No tracking of job execution time by tier/type
Missing metrics on script regeneration success/failure
No monitoring of cost estimation accuracy vs. actual spending
Missing alerts for stuck CHECKPOINT jobs
No tracking of step failure rates (which steps fail most)

businessValue.rationale: Core video content generation system. Manages entire lifecycle from concept to production with cost controls and quality gates.
businessValue.userImpact: Admins can create video concepts, preview at low cost (nano), then scale to production (draft/final) with cost transparency.

compositeGoal.name: Video Concept Production Quality
compositeGoal.target: <2h preview cycle, >95% production success, <5% cost variance, >4.5/5.0 quality

features.name: Video Generation
features.description: Video asset storage and management: create videos from concept snapshots and script data, store tier (NANO/DRAFT/FINAL), status lifecycle (GENERATING → DRAFT → READY → SCHEDULED → PUBLISHED → ARCHIVED), duration/aspect ratio tracking, R2 storage for video files and thumbnails, presigned upload URLs for client uploads, asset confirmation with R2 head checks.

issues: Slug validation regex no centralized (inline in schema)
Asset deletion happens in parallel without transaction (partial cleanup on failure)
No validation that videoR2Key/thumbnailR2Key actually exist in R2 before storing

gaps: No tracking of video generation time by tier
Missing metrics on asset upload failures
No monitoring of R2 storage usage (costs)
Missing alerts for stale GENERATING status
No tracking of conceptSlug linkage/orphaned videos

businessValue.rationale: Enables multi-tier video production (nano drafts → final production). Integrates with video pipeline for content creation at scale.
businessValue.userImpact: Admins can manage video assets across production tiers with storage isolation and cost tracking.

compositeGoal.name: Video Asset Management
compositeGoal.target: >99% upload success, <500GB peak usage, <2min confirmation time, <$500/month R2

features.name: Lead Generation
features.description: Influencer/creator lead management system with full lifecycle: NEW → VERIFIED → OUTREACH_READY → CONTACTED → REPLIED → ENGAGED → CONVERTED or REJECTED/STALE/BLOCKED. Bulk import via CSV with deduplication, lead assignment to staff, status transition validation, CSV export, API-key-only lead management endpoints for pipeline automation.

issues: Status transition validation hardcoded in constants (no database-driven config)
Two separate endpoints for list (JWT staff) vs. (API key) with mostly duplicate code
CSV export builds rows manually without library (fragile quote escaping)
Capability check (LEAD_GEN) done in custom middleware instead of centralized
No validation on platforms array length or uniqueness

gaps: No metrics on lead conversion funnel (drop-off at each stage)
Missing tracking of outreach success rates by channel
No monitoring of staff assignment distribution (workload balance)
Missing metrics on deduplication effectiveness
No SLA tracking for lead processing time

businessValue.rationale: Core pipeline for discovering and engaging influencers/creators for partnerships and business development. Direct impact on partnership revenue.
businessValue.userImpact: Staff can systematically discover, evaluate, and contact leads with tracking of engagement status. API enables automated pipeline integration.

compositeGoal.name: Lead Generation Effectiveness
compositeGoal.target: >200 leads/month, >8% conversion, <30 days avg, >95% contact valid

features.name: Moderation Queue
features.description: Content moderation workflow for flagged human profiles and agent reports. Manual review queue with status transitions (PENDING → REVIEWED → DISMISSED/BLOCKED). Spam detection via OpenAI moderation API. Action tracking with reason logging. Admin dashboard with filtering by status/reason.

issues: OpenAI moderation API call not cached (repeated calls for same content waste budget)
No rate limiting on report submission (spam vulnerability)
Report reason enum could be extended without code changes (should be config)
Action taken stored as free text (should be enum for consistency)

existingMetrics: Report counts by status and reason
gaps: No metrics on moderation review latency
Missing tracking of false positive report rates
No monitoring of action effectiveness (re-reports of same account)
Missing alerts for high-volume report spikes
No SLA tracking for urgent review (e.g., FRAUD reports)

businessValue.rationale: Maintains platform integrity and safety. Prevents spam, fraud, harassment, and abuse. Essential for user trust and legal compliance.
businessValue.userImpact: Users can report problematic accounts/agents. Admins can review and take action (block, suspend, warn).

compositeGoal.name: Moderation Effectiveness
compositeGoal.target: <4h review time, >88% correct decisions, >85% recidivism prevention, >4.2/5.0 trust rating

features.name: Time Tracking
features.description: Staff clock in/out system with time entry logging, hourly rate configuration, hours adjustment request/approval workflow, payment tracking, balance calculation (earned - paid). Summary stats for today/week/month. Admin view of all staff with daily rates and clock status.

issues: Date math for weekly/monthly ranges duplicated (should be shared utility)
Decimal type used for staffDailyRate but converted to Number throughout (type mismatch)
Raw SQL in staff-stats endpoint (parameterized but complex logic)
No validation on duration ceiling (could be exploited for huge hours)

gaps: No metrics on clock-out latency or forgotten clock-outs
Missing alerts for staff with abnormal hours (>12h/day, <2h/day)
No tracking of adjustment approval rates
Missing metrics on payment processing time (balance → paid)
No audit trail for rate/payment changes

businessValue.rationale: Essential for staff payroll and labor cost management. Enables transparent tracking of hours and payment obligations.
businessValue.userImpact: Staff can track hours, request adjustments, and see earned/owed balance. Admins can process payments and enforce rate policies.

compositeGoal.name: Time Tracking Accuracy
compositeGoal.target: >99% entry completion, 100% reconciliation, <24h adjustment approval, >4.2/5.0 satisfaction

features.name: Watchdog & Error Monitor
features.description: Automated error monitoring system that scans PM2 logs for error patterns, categorizes by fingerprint, counts occurrences, and surfaces high-frequency/high-severity errors to admins. Claude AI analysis of error causes and potential fixes. Auto-fix proposal, branch creation, testing, and approval workflow for non-critical errors. Manual fix approval required for FATAL errors.

issues: Auto-fix logic split across multiple modules (error-autofix.js) with unclear separation
Test error endpoint allows arbitrary message injection (security risk)
Status validation hardcoded in endpoint (should be enum)
Trend bucketing uses string key slicing (brittle date parsing)
Raw error fingerprinting logic (no normalization of similar error messages)
No circuit breaker for Claude API calls (could exhaust budget on error spike)

gaps: No metrics on Claude analysis accuracy vs. manual review
Missing tracking of auto-fix success/failure rates
No monitoring of error resolution time
Missing alerts for new error categories
No tracking of false positives in fingerprinting

businessValue.rationale: Detects and helps fix production errors in real-time without manual incident response. Improves uptime and reduces MTTR through automated diagnosis.
businessValue.userImpact: Admins receive alerts for critical errors with AI-proposed fixes. Automated testing and staging reduces manual review burden.

compositeGoal.name: Error Detection & Resolution
compositeGoal.target: <5min detection, >85% analysis accuracy, >75% fix success, <5% false positives

features.name: Agent Abuse Reports
features.description: Humans report agents for SPAM/FRAUD/HARASSMENT/IRRELEVANT/OTHER with optional description. Rate limited 3/day per human. POST /api/agents/:id/report accepts JWT or email report token. abuseScore incremented atomically. Auto-suspension at 3+ reports, auto-ban at 5+. Admin reviews reports, can DISMISS.

issues: Auto-suspension/ban thresholds (3 and 5) hard-coded, not configurable
No appeal mechanism for suspended/banned agents
DISMISSED status only available to admin, no documented admin API
abuseScore can grow unbounded (no decay/forgiveness over time)
Email report token JWT includes agentId + jobId, no rate limiting on token generation

gaps: No report reason distribution
Missing false positive rate tracking
No suspension/ban outcomes tracking
Missing reporter identity verification metrics

businessValue.rationale: Platform safety critical. Prevents abuse and protects user trust.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans can report malicious agents. Bad actors are removed.

features.name: Agent Domain Verification
features.description: Agents verify domain ownership via well-known file (DNS TXT record) or HTTP endpoint (.well-known/humanpages-verify.txt). Stores verification token on registration, checked against agent.websiteUrl.

issues: 10s timeout on fetch/DNS may be too aggressive for slow networks
No retry logic for transient DNS failures
DNS and HTTP verification methods not equally robust (DNS more reliable but oEmbed-style tricks could bypass)

gaps: No tracking of verification success/failure rates
No metrics on verification method usage
Missing timeout/network failure tracking

businessValue.rationale: Builds trust signal but optional. Agents auto-activate without it. Low friction preferred.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can display domain-verified badge for credibility

features.name: Agent Payment Activation
features.description: Agents can pay $5 USD (configurable) in USDC/USDT/DAI on Ethereum/Base/Polygon/Arbitrum to activate PRO tier (60 days). POST /payment returns deposit address + instructions. POST /payment/verify checks on-chain tx via verifyActivationPayment(), prevents txHash reuse.

issues: verifyActivationPayment imported dynamically without type safety
No handling of stale payments (txHash valid but old, outside grace period)
Missing configuration validation for AGENT_ACTIVATION_DEPOSIT_ADDRESS
Error handling defers to PaymentVerificationError which may not cover all cases

gaps: No payment success/failure tracking
No token/network distribution analytics
Missing deposit address balance monitoring

businessValue.rationale: Direct revenue generator + alternative to social verification. Targets serious agents willing to pay.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents with liquidity can skip social verification. Immediate PRO tier.

features.name: Agent Registration
features.description: Agents register with name, optional description/website/contact, auto-activate as PRO tier. Generates hp_ API key, verification token, assigns sequential ERC-8004 agentId. SSRF validation for webhook URLs.

issues: No explicit API key rotation mechanism
Webhook URL validation could be more restrictive (private IPs blocked but loopback edge cases possible)

gaps: No metrics for registration failures
No API key generation/usage tracking
Missing webhook delivery failures

businessValue.rationale: First touchpoint for agent onboarding. Auto-PRO tier removal of friction dramatically improves conversion.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can start posting jobs immediately without payment/verification barriers

features.name: Agent Social Activation
features.description: Agents activate by posting to social platforms (Twitter/LinkedIn/TikTok/YouTube/Telegram/Moltbook, Facebook/Instagram if META_APP_ACCESS_TOKEN set). System generates 24h verification code, verifies post via oEmbed/API, extracts follower count to determine tier (BASIC/PRO/WHALE). Auto-determines expiry based on tier.

issues: Platform-specific oEmbed parsing logic is repetitive (Twitter/LinkedIn/TikTok/YouTube all similar)
Follower count extraction not fully implemented (Twitter oEmbed returns null for count)
Moltbook API integration assumes post endpoint structure without versioning
Meta oEmbed requires explicit feature flag + access token, no graceful degradation

gaps: No per-platform success rate tracking
Missing failed verification attempt metrics
No code reuse/exploit detection
No follower count distribution analysis

businessValue.rationale: Sybil resistance + viral loop. Cost of creating fake social accounts prevents abuse. Follower count tiers incentivize marketing.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents with large followings get better tier/limits. Free marketing for HumanPages on social platforms.

features.name: Job Applications (Listings)
features.description: Humans apply to listings with optional pitch (500 chars). Application stored with PENDING status, listingSnapshot captured. Agents review applications, can offer (creates Job), reject, or request reconfirm if listing terms change. Reconfirm flow for PENDING_RECONFIRM status.

issues: Material field list (MATERIAL_FIELDS const) is hardcoded, not validated against Prisma schema
Snapshot JSON structure not validated on updates, could cause comparison failures
Reconfirm flow does not email humans proactively, only stores flag

gaps: No application-to-offer conversion metrics
Missing rejection reason tracking
No reconfirm acceptance rate metrics

businessValue.rationale: Central to job-board matching workflow. Applications = engagement signal.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans can apply without custom job creation. Agents filter candidates.

features.name: Job Board & Search
features.description: Public GET /api/listings with filters (category, location, radius, skills, budget range, verified agents, pro agents). Pagination. Geosearch with haversine distance. Short link codes for campaign tracking (6-char codes, 10 per listing). Rate limited 30/min.

issues: Link code collision retry logic assumes 5 attempts is sufficient (rare but possible)
Radius filtering requires both lat/lng, no fallback to city-level matching
Filter combinations can result in 0 results with no suggestions for refinement
No full-text search, only exact category match

existingMetrics: Link code click tracking via clicks counter
gaps: No search query analytics
Missing filter usage breakdown
No radius distribution metrics
Missing time-to-apply funnel

businessValue.rationale: Drives human acquisition and matching. Campaign tracking via link codes helps optimize marketing.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans discover opportunities via search. Agents measure campaign ROI via short codes.

features.name: Job Creation by Agents
features.description: Authenticated agents create job offers with title, description, priceUsdc, optional paymentMode (ONE_TIME/STREAM), streamMethod/interval/rate, location, category. Rate-limited by tier (BASIC 10/day, PRO 100/day, WHALE 500/day). Stored in PENDING status. Webhook callback optional.

issues: IP-based rate limiting may be bypassed by proxies despite X-Forwarded-For parsing
No duplicate offer detection (same human + agent may see multiple PENDING offers)
Stream payment validation deferred to stream-specific endpoints, not validated at creation
No geographic distance validation between agent location and job location

gaps: No offer creation success metrics
Missing moderation queue tracking
No per-category volume analytics
No stream vs one-time breakdown

businessValue.rationale: Core revenue driver. Every offer represents a potential transaction and fees.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can immediately post work. Humans can discover opportunities.

features.name: Job Lifecycle (One-Time)
features.description: One-time payment jobs flow: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PAID (or PAYMENT_CLAIMED→confirmed) → COMPLETED. Upfront or upon-completion timing. Human accepts (PATCH /accept), agent marks PAID with txHash (PATCH /paid or /claim-payment + /confirm-payment off-chain), human submits work (PATCH /complete), agent approves (PATCH /approve-completion). Cancellation and dispute at any stage.

issues: Status transition logic not centralized (each endpoint checks independently)
No explicit guard rails for impossible transitions (e.g., COMPLETED → ACCEPTED)
Off-chain payment claim requires secondary confirmation, no timeout/expiry
Dispute payload structure not validated (reason field is free text)

gaps: No timing/duration per status analytics
Missing payment method distribution (on-chain vs off-chain)
No dispute reason tracking
Missing cancellation reason analytics

businessValue.rationale: Complete job lifecycle = revenue realization and customer satisfaction metric.
businessValue.userImpact: Clear workflow for both agents and humans to complete work and receive payment.

features.name: Job Lifecycle (Streaming)
features.description: Streaming payment jobs: PENDING → ACCEPTED → STREAMING (Superfluid flow or micro-transfers) → PAUSED (on missed ticks) → COMPLETED. Interval-based (HOURLY/DAILY/WEEKLY), rate in USDC, max ticks optional. StreamTick records verify each payment, grace period for misses.

issues: Superfluid and micro-transfer logic mixed in same Job model (different verification paths)
StreamTick PENDING status never transitioned to VERIFIED for Superfluid (relies on cron)
No auto-pause on grace period exceeded (requires external monitor cron)
Decimal precision for streamFlowRate stored as string (int96) but no validation
No test coverage for Superfluid flow rate calculation edge cases

gaps: No flow rate distribution metrics
Missing active stream count
No grace period exceeded tracking
Missing Superfluid vs micro-transfer breakdown

businessValue.rationale: Recurring revenue model. Appeals to agents wanting ongoing engagement.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can offer retainers/hourly work. Humans get predictable recurring income.

features.name: Listing Creation
features.description: Agents create job-board listings (different from direct job offers). POST /api/listings with title, description, budget, required skills/equipment, location, workMode, maxApplicants. Generates 10 short ListingLink codes (campaign tracking). Material field changes trigger application reconfirm flow. Image upload optional (cover image).

issues: Material field snapshot comparison relies on JSON string equality (whitespace/order sensitive)
Image upload allows multer/sharp processing but no format/size limits validated upfront
isPro/isVerified denormalized from agent at creation, stale if agent status changes
Location geocoding optional, some filters require lat/lng

gaps: No creation success/failure breakdown
Missing update frequency analytics
No application volume per listing
Missing image upload failure tracking

businessValue.rationale: Alternative to direct job offers. Job board model enables bulk hiring and campaign tracking.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can post reusable listings. Humans can browse organized opportunities.

features.name: MCP HTTP Transport
features.description: Streamable HTTP transport for MCP. POST /mcp for JSON-RPC messages (initialize, tools/list, tools/call, notifications). GET /mcp for server-initiated notifications via SSE. DELETE /mcp to terminate session. Session management via Mcp-Session-Id header. Max 10K sessions, 60min timeout, LRU eviction, SSE 5min max duration.

issues: In-memory session store (same as OAuth) loses state on restart
SSE 5min max could cut off long-running operations
No backpressure handling if client doesn't consume SSE messages quickly
CORS locked to FRONTEND_URL but SSE may come from different origin

gaps: No active session count metrics
Missing message throughput tracking
No SSE duration distribution
Missing tool execution time metrics

businessValue.rationale: Infrastructure for MCP clients (GPT plugins, etc). Enables automation integrations.
businessValue.userImpact: Developers can build chatbot integrations. Agents can automate workflows.

features.name: MCP OAuth 2.1
features.description: Implements OAuth 2.0/2.1 endpoints for GPT developer connectors. RFC 6749 (framework), RFC 7591 (dynamic client registration), RFC 7636 (PKCE S256 mandatory), RFC 8707 (resource indicators), RFC 7009 (revocation). In-memory stores with size caps (5K clients, 10K auth codes, 10K refresh tokens). Auth codes expire 5min, refresh tokens 30 days. Security: timing-safe secrets, PKCE S256 only (plain disallowed), HTML escaping, generic errors.

issues: In-memory stores will lose state on restart (production should use persistent store)
Max map size caps (5K, 10K) may be too small for large deployments
LRU eviction happens at size cap, could drop valid active tokens
No explicit PKCE code challenge validation before token request

gaps: No OAuth flow success/failure tracking
Missing client registration rate
No token lifetime distribution
Missing PKCE validation failure metrics

businessValue.rationale: Enables GPT integrations and third-party MCP clients. Expands ecosystem.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can connect via OAuth without sharing API keys. Developers can build integrations.

features.name: Off-Chain Payment Claims
features.description: Agents claim off-chain payments (PayPal/Venmo/Wise/etc) with reference number or proof link. PATCH /jobs/:id/claim-payment stores claimMethod and claimNote. Humans confirm via PATCH /jobs/:id/confirm-payment. Job moves PAYMENT_CLAIMED → PAID. No on-chain verification; relies on human trust.

issues: No validation of claimMethod against VALID_PAYMENT_PREFERENCES list
claimNote is free text, no structured evidence (no link validation or screenshot capture)
No timeout on PAYMENT_CLAIMED state (can sit indefinitely if human doesn't confirm)
No audit trail of who rejected vs accepted the claim
Dispute handling for off-chain claims identical to on-chain (may need different resolution logic)

gaps: No off-chain payment method distribution
Missing claim confirmation time analytics
No dispute rate by payment method
Missing fraud pattern detection

businessValue.rationale: Fallback for agents without crypto wallets. Lowers friction but higher fraud risk.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents can claim payment via traditional methods. Humans remain responsible for validation.

features.name: On-Chain Payment Verification
features.description: Verify USDC transfers on-chain (Ethereum/Base/Polygon/Arbitrum). Check transaction receipt, recipient wallet(s), amount, token, confirmations (100+ required). Return PaymentVerificationError on failure. Used in PATCH /jobs/:id/paid and StreamTick verification.

issues: Hard-coded 100 confirmation requirement may be too strict for active chains
No mempool/pending transaction detection (user can't check until confirmed)
Multi-token support (USDC vs USDT vs DAI) not fully tested

gaps: No verification success/failure breakdown by network
Missing confirmation time analytics
No gas fee tracking

businessValue.rationale: Core trust mechanism. Prevents scams and ensures payment integrity.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans trust that payments are legitimately confirmed on-chain.

features.name: Reviews & Reputation
features.description: After job COMPLETED, agents can leave 1-5 star review + optional comment. Review captures ERC-8004 pre-computed values (erc8004Value, erc8004Tag1/Tag2, feedbackHash SHA-256). GET /api/agents/:id returns computed reputation (totalJobs, completedJobs, paidJobs, avgPaymentSpeedHours). Human can respond (single reply, Google Maps style).

issues: ERC-8004 feedback hash computed but never broadcast to on-chain contract (future bridge dependency)
No review authenticity check (agent could have not actually completed the work)
Comment length not enforced (no max chars field in schema)
Response text limited to 2000 chars but no comment length limit creates asymmetry

gaps: No review distribution (star breakdown)
Missing review sentiment analysis
No time-to-review metrics
Missing response rate by agent

businessValue.rationale: Reputation system drives trust. ERC-8004 standard enables on-chain portability.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents build public reputation. Humans can assess agent reliability.

features.name: Solver Verification
features.description: Sybil-resistant verification for agents. Email, GitHub, Telegram, or wallet verification. Email: OTP sent, verified via token. GitHub: OAuth redirect to GitHub, extract user ID. Telegram: Bot challenge, user confirms. Wallet: Sign message with EIP-191. Prevents identity reuse (solverGithubId/solverTelegramId unique). Flag solverVerified + solverVerifiedMethod on agent.

issues: No test coverage at all
Email verification uses JWT with dynamic payload, no token rotation
GitHub OAuth callback assumes state parameter, no CSRF token validation explicit in code
Telegram verification assumes bot webhook, implementation incomplete
Wallet signature message format not validated against standard EIP-191 prefix
No rate limiting on per-method attempts (email/GitHub/Telegram sent separately)

gaps: No verification method distribution
Missing failed attempt tracking
No identity reuse detection metrics
Missing verification time analytics

businessValue.rationale: Prevents sybil attacks and repeat offenders. Critical for platform safety.
businessValue.userImpact: Verified agents get trust signals. Banned agents harder to rebuild on new identity.

features.name: Active Users Chart (DAU/WAU/MAU)
features.description: Daily active users, weekly active users, and monthly active users with DAU/WAU ratio and retention rate. Powered by SignupAnalyticsChart with time series, conversion rates, and stacked breakdown views. Includes 7-day moving averages, period-over-period trends, and applications-per-job metrics.

issues: Rolling average calculation (rollingAvg) is pure but could be extracted to utils
Retention rate calculation (signupToActive) has implicit logic that is not self-documenting
No edge case handling for users with no lastActiveAt
DAU/WAU/MAU definitions hardcoded (24h, 7d, 30d) - not configurable

gaps: No cohort-based retention (e.g., day-1, day-7, day-30 retention by signup cohort)
No churn alerts (declining DAU/WAU/MAU)
Missing session duration or depth metrics
No channel-specific retention
No user segmentation (verified vs unverified, premium vs free)

businessValue.rationale: Core engagement metric for SaaS health. DAU/WAU/MAU trends indicate product-market fit and user satisfaction. Retention rate drives LTV calculations.
businessValue.userImpact: Exec team: track platform stickiness and growth health. Product: validate feature impact on retention.

features.name: Admin Agent Management
features.description: Paginated agent list (20 per page) with search, status filter (ACTIVE/PENDING/SUSPENDED/BANNED), and 8 sortable columns: name, status, tier, jobs, reports, abuse score, last active, registered date. Shows verification badge, domain status, abuse strikes, and website links.

issues: Client-side sorting function (sortAgents) duplicates backend logic
No frontend tests for AdminAgents component
Abuse score calculation is opaque (no formula documented)
Status colors hardcoded in UI - should come from backend
Missing pagination for 1000+ agents (list could be huge)
No bulk operations (e.g., suspend all agents from domain X)

gaps: No agent activation funnel tracking
Missing abuse score methodology documentation
No alerts for tier upgrade/downgrade events
No agent cohort analysis (when activated, retention)
Missing agent onboarding time metrics

businessValue.rationale: Enables agent quality management, abuse detection, and tier governance. Critical for marketplace health.
businessValue.userImpact: Marketplace team: review and suspend bad actors. Support: investigate agent complaints.

compositeGoal.name: Agent Quality Index
compositeGoal.target: 85%+ active agents, <5% banned

features.name: Admin Job Management
features.description: Paginated job list (20 per page) with search and status filter (PENDING, ACCEPTED, SUBMITTED, PAYMENT_CLAIMED, PAID, COMPLETED, REJECTED, CANCELLED, DISPUTED). Shows job title, status, human name, agent name, price in USDC, payment details, and creation date. Supports linking to both humans and agents.

issues: No frontend tests
Status colors hardcoded in component
Job price display shows 'Unpaid' but should show why (cancelled, rejected, etc.)
No dispute resolution UI (disputes are shown but not actionable)
Missing bulk job operations (e.g., refund all DISPUTED jobs)
Agent name can come from agentId or agentName - inconsistent

gaps: No job funnel (created → accepted → submitted → paid)
Missing dispute resolution time metrics
No job quality metrics (refund rate, late jobs)
Missing marketplace health indicators (fill rate, avg job value)
No job type attribution (which agents/humans create which types)

businessValue.rationale: Core marketplace operation. Required for dispute resolution, fraud detection, and payment reconciliation.
businessValue.userImpact: Support: resolve disputes and payment issues. Finance: reconcile payments. Marketplace ops: quality control.

compositeGoal.name: Marketplace Health
compositeGoal.target: 70%+ jobs completed and paid

features.name: Admin Moderation Queue
features.description: Content moderation interface for job postings, profile photos, human reports, and agent reports. Paginated queue (20 per page) with status filter (pending, approved, rejected, error) and content type filter. Shows content ID, attempts, error messages, created/reviewed timestamps, and inline approve/reject buttons. Manual override of automated moderation decisions.

issues: Content type update logic hardcoded in PATCH handler (profile_photo updates Human, job_posting updates Job, etc.)
Error handling uses .catch(() => {}) to silently fail when source record is deleted
No frontend tests for AdminModeration component
Content type labels hardcoded in UI
No escalation workflow (e.g., 3 rejected items = auto-ban user)
Missing audit trail for who approved/rejected what

existingMetrics: Moderation queue counts by status (pending, approved, rejected, error)
gaps: No moderation speed metrics (time to approval)
Missing false positive rate tracking
No content type metrics (which types have highest error rate)
No reviewer performance analytics (who approves/rejects, speed, accuracy)
Missing escalation alerts (N pending items, oldest item age)

businessValue.rationale: Critical for compliance, user safety, and legal risk mitigation. Enables human review of automated moderation decisions.
businessValue.userImpact: Trust & safety team: review and override moderation decisions. Legal: compliance audit trail.

compositeGoal.name: Moderation SLA
compositeGoal.target: 100% of critical items reviewed within 24h, 95% within 48h

features.name: Admin Overview KPIs
features.description: Aggregate dashboard with 50+ metrics across users, agents, jobs, listings, reports, affiliates, feedback, and platform insights including DAU/WAU/MAU, retention, profile completeness, crypto wallet adoption, verification methods, work mode, geographic distribution, and top skills.

issues: SQL queries are parameterized but duplicated across multiple endpoints (could extract into lib functions)
Error handling catches all exceptions generically without specific types
Heavy reliance on raw SQL for analytics (maintainability risk)
No caching layer for frequently-accessed stats (may impact performance)

existingMetrics: Agent status breakdown (ACTIVE, PENDING, SUSPENDED, BANNED)
Job status breakdown (COMPLETED, PAID, ACCEPTED, PENDING, REJECTED, DISPUTED)
gaps: No real-time session tracking (relies on lastActiveAt)
Missing feature flag analytics (PostHog integration not implemented)
No cohort analysis for churn prediction
Limited behavioral event tracking
No A/B test attribution

businessValue.rationale: Provides exec visibility into platform health, growth velocity, user engagement, and revenue metrics in real-time. Core decision-making dashboard for strategy, marketing attribution, product prioritization.
businessValue.userImpact: Admin team (3-5 people): makes data-driven decisions on growth initiatives, feature prioritization, and compliance. Product team gets behavioral insights on profile completion and wallet adoption.

features.name: Signup Analytics Timeseries
features.description: Growth Analytics with 16+ customizable time series: signups, verified, CV uploads, crypto wallet connections, jobs, paid jobs, payment volume, applications, agents, plus conversion rates (verified %, crypto %, CV %, apps/job) and 7-day moving averages. Supports 7/14/30/60/90-day windows and three view modes (time series, conversion rates, stacked breakdown).

issues: Series config (SERIES array) is very large (86 lines) - could split into separate concerns
View mode logic (timeseries/rates/stacked) creates tight coupling in render
Rolling average calculation doesn't handle sparse data well
safeDiv utility is simple but could use better naming (divideOrZero?)
Right Y-axis auto-detection heuristic (5x threshold) is magic-numbered
Tooltip component is 270 lines and does too much

gaps: No weekly/monthly aggregations (only daily + rolling avg)
Missing anomaly detection (e.g., alert on 50% drop)
No seasonal decomposition (trend vs seasonality)
Limited forecasting (no trend projections)
No variance analysis (target vs actual)
No external factor tracking (marketing campaigns, feature launches)

businessValue.rationale: Enables trend analysis and early warning signs for growth stalls. Multi-metric view helps isolate bottlenecks (e.g., signups up but conversions down suggests onboarding issue).
businessValue.userImpact: Growth & marketing: track campaign effectiveness, channel quality, and velocity. Product: validate impact of features on conversion.

compositeGoal.name: Growth Velocity Index
compositeGoal.target: 130% or higher

features.name: Signup Funnel Analytics
features.description: Multi-stage funnel analysis tracking user progression through: signed up → email verified → profile started → CV uploaded → wallet connected → photo uploaded → full profile 80%+. Includes source quality metrics, signup method breakdown (email/Google/LinkedIn/WhatsApp), user abandonment cohorts, onboarding velocity (median hours to milestones), and weekly cohort funnel analysis.

issues: Complex SQL queries with CASE/WHEN logic repeated (abandonment, velocity)
No frontend tests for SignupFunnelChart component
Frontend component tightly couples data fetching with presentation
Query logic for profile completeness calculation duplicated in main stats endpoint
Missing null checks on optional numeric fields (medianHoursToCv, medianHoursToActive)

existingMetrics: Source quality (UTM source conversion rates, last 90d)
Velocity metrics (median hours to active/CV, avg completeness by window)
gaps: No daily funnel dropoff rates (only cohort-level)
Missing device/browser attribution for abandonment
No funnel re-engagement tracking (users who return after abandonment)
Limited attribution (UTM only, no referral source)
No variant/experiment tracking for funnel optimization

businessValue.rationale: Identifies where users drop off in onboarding (critical for growth). Source quality data enables marketing spend optimization. Velocity metrics validate product changes.
businessValue.userImpact: Growth team: optimize acquisition channels and onboarding flow. Product: measures impact of UX changes on funnel conversion.

compositeGoal.name: Funnel Health Score
compositeGoal.target: 70% overall, 85% for top 3 sources

features.name: Admin User Management
features.description: Paginated user list (20 per page, 100 limit) with search (name, email, username), filters (verified/unverified, catch-all/regular), and sortable columns (name, email, createdAt, lastActiveAt). Shows job count, review count, referral link copy, and verification status. Supports 'Default Humans' (catch-all) special role.

issues: Frontend doesn't have unit tests
Pagination hardcoded to 20 items - not user-configurable
Filter logic (email, name, username) duplicated in backend search
Sort field whitelist is good but could be extracted to constants
No rate limiting on search/filter endpoints
Search is case-insensitive but uses 'insensitive' mode - performance may degrade with large result sets

gaps: No audit log for user modifications
No tracking of who viewed/modified which users
Missing bulk operation tracking
No alerts for suspicious user accounts (e.g., created then immediately banned)
No user status change history

businessValue.rationale: Enables compliance review, support escalations, and fraud investigation. Catch-all user role is critical for testing.
businessValue.userImpact: Support team: find and review specific users. Admin: manage special accounts (test accounts, staff accounts).

compositeGoal.name: User Management Efficiency
compositeGoal.target: 90% of lookups succeed in ≤3 searches

features.name: Vouch Event Metrics
features.description: Vouch (referral/endorsement) event tracking integrated into admin overview. Shows counts of users who have connected via referral links and vouch transaction volumes.
backendRoutes: GET /api/admin/stats (includes vouch metrics in future extensions)
backendFiles: src/routes/admin.ts (not yet implemented - mentioned in types)

issues: Feature not yet implemented in admin analytics
No vouch-specific endpoints in admin routes
No test coverage for vouch metrics
No frontend UI for vouch analytics

gaps: No vouch volume tracking
No acceptance rate metrics
Missing vouch funnel (sent → accepted → used)
No influencer identification (top vouchers)
No vouch ROI tracking (users who join via vouch vs organic)

businessValue.rationale: Referral program is secondary. Vouch metrics would help optimize viral growth, but not critical for core platform.
businessValue.userImpact: Growth team: optimize referral incentives. Product: measure viral coefficient.

compositeGoal.name: Referral Velocity
compositeGoal.target: 0.2+ per active user per month

features.name: Crypto Wallet Connection
features.description: Signature-verified wallet connection with automatic multi-chain registration across EVM mainnet networks (Ethereum, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum).

issues: Rate limiting applied to wallet creation (20 per hour) but not to GET operations
Address normalization uses lowercase; EIP-55 checksums not validated but explicitly documented
Nonce-based verification uses simple in-memory store (nonceStore) without TTL clarity in code
Privy identity token verification has fallback to REST fetch but error handling could be clearer
Manual and signature-based wallet flows share similar logic but differ in verified flag logic
Two separate POST routes (/api/wallets and /api/wallets/manual) handle different verification methods with overlapping concerns

existingMetrics: logger.error on all error paths with context
logger.warn for Privy DID conflicts and verification failures
Analytics track: wallet_connect_started, wallet_connect_success, wallet_sign_rejected, wallet_sign_failed
gaps: No success/failure rate metrics for nonce generation
No metrics for multi-chain automatic registration (how many users register 1 vs 4 networks)
No tracking of address normalization failures or EIP-55 mismatches
No metrics for Privy DID binding success rate
Missing instrumentation for signature verification latency
No alerts for spike in invalid signatures or nonce reuse attempts

businessValue.rationale: Crypto wallets are the primary payment mechanism for the platform; enables agents to pay users directly on-chain. Direct revenue dependency: job payments flow to verified wallets.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans need verified wallets to receive crypto payments from agents. Seamless multi-chain support reduces friction. Privy integration enables non-technical users via email/Google signup.

features.name: Fiat Payment Methods
features.description: Off-chain fiat payment handles (Wise, Venmo, PayPal, Cash App, Revolut, Zelle, Monzo, N26, Mercado Pago) stored as user-keyed records; supports primary method designation.

issues: No unit tests exist for fiat payment method CRUD operations
Handle validation uses simple trim + lowercase transformation; no platform-specific validation (e.g., Venmo usernames vs @handles)
Platform enum hardcoded in code (PLATFORMS const) instead of deriving from schema; would require code change to add new platform
Primary method selection uses transaction but doesn't validate that at least one method remains primary after operations
Duplicate detection via unique([humanId, platform, handle]) constraint; error message could be more helpful (which platform/handle duplicated)
No rate limit differentiation; all fiat method operations (read/write) share same 20-per-hour limit
Handle stored lowercase but no documentation on case sensitivity for actual payment transfers

gaps: No analytics events tracking fiat method additions/removals
Missing metrics on platform distribution (which platforms most used)
No instrumentation for duplicate handle attempts (fraud/spam indicator)
Missing tracking of primary method changes (user behavior signal)
No alerts for unusual handle patterns or suspicious adds
Missing latency metrics for fiat method operations

businessValue.rationale: Enables off-chain fiat payments for agents to pay humans; critical for users in regions without crypto adoption. Supports ZKP2P and other on/off-ramps.
businessValue.userImpact: Users can receive payments via familiar platforms (Venmo, PayPal, Wise); reduces crypto friction. Primary method selection improves UX.

features.name: Payment Onboarding Step
features.description: Multi-step onboarding UI component integrating crypto wallet connection (Privy), manual address paste, and fiat payment method setup in single flow.

issues: No frontend tests exist for StepPayment component
Lazy-loaded PrivyWalletConnect to avoid bundling @privy-io/react-auth; increases code complexity and lazy-loading waterfall
EVM address validation duplicated: isValidEvmAddress called in frontend before backend validation
Error state management uses string-based errors; no structured error codes for different failure types
loadMethods silently fails with console.log in catch block; errors not reported to user
Fiat payment method list fetched from profile endpoint but persisted via separate fiatPaymentMethods endpoint; potential stale data issue
No transaction semantics: if wallet succeeds but fiat method fails, user gets incomplete onboarding
Skip button not shown but onNext allows incomplete profile; users can skip payment setup

existingMetrics: Analytics track wallet_connect_started, wallet_connect_success, wallet_sign_rejected, wallet_sign_failed
gaps: No instrumentation for fiat method addition during onboarding
Missing metrics on step completion rate (what % of users complete payment setup)
No tracking of skip rate or which methods users skip
Missing instrumentation for time spent on payment step
No A/B metrics comparing Privy vs manual paste adoption
Missing funnel metrics: started → connected → verified → completed
No alerts for high error rates on wallet connection

businessValue.rationale: Payment setup is critical onboarding gate; users cannot receive jobs without wallet or fiat method. Direct impact on activation rate and initial revenue potential.
businessValue.userImpact: First-time experience with payment infrastructure; friction here directly correlates with signup-to-job-receive conversion. Multi-method support accommodates diverse user preferences.

features.name: Privy Embedded Wallet
features.description: Email/Google-based wallet creation and verification via Privy identity tokens; wallets verified through Privy API without requiring signature.

issues: Identity token verification happens during onboarding but is async; logout may interrupt token verification flow
Privy DID unique constraint error (P2002) converted to 409 but error message could be clearer about cross-account binding
Fallback to getPrivyUserByDid REST fetch when identity token is stale; no backoff logic if REST API fails
Mobile-specific wallet creation logic (createOnLogin:'off') creates conditional behavior not tested in unit tests
handledRef pattern used in PrivyWalletConnect to prevent duplicate registration but relies on ref mutation which could be fragile
Identity token expiry not explicitly documented; assumes short TTL but no comment explaining why

existingMetrics: logger.error and logger.warn for Privy DID conflicts, identity token verification failures
gaps: No metrics tracking Privy identity token verification latency
Missing success rate for embedded wallet creation vs external wallet signup
No instrumentation for REST API fallback usage (how often is getPrivyUserByDid called)
Missing mobile vs desktop wallet creation rate comparison
No alerts for Privy API availability issues
Missing tracking of 409 Privy DID conflicts (indicator of account takeover attempts)

businessValue.rationale: Embedded wallets lower activation friction for non-crypto users; enables web2-to-web3 onboarding via email/Google. Critical for accessibility but not strictly required (fallback: manual address paste).
businessValue.userImpact: Non-technical users can create wallets without wallet software; reduces abandonment during payment setup. Mobile-first design improves conversion on phones.

features.name: Wallet-Fiat Overlap & Integration Points
features.description: Interaction between wallet and fiat payment methods: wallet verification status affects job payment routing; fiat methods used as fallback for off-chain settlements; primary wallet/method selection for agent payment preference.

issues: No explicit integration between wallet and fiat payment routes; separate endpoints without cross-validation
Job payment claiming accepts paymentClaimMethod field but doesn't validate against user's fiat methods (could claim PayPal when user has no PayPal handle)
No backend logic to enforce primary wallet/method selection; frontend only
isPrimary flag exists on both Wallet and FiatPaymentMethod but no coordination; user could have primary wallet unverified while fiat method verified
Payment routing logic not visible in audited files; unclear how payment settlement chooses between wallet vs fiat method
No transaction constraints; user could delete all verified wallets and all fiat methods simultaneously, becoming unpayable
Overlap concept not documented; unclear whether both are required or either/or; Job schema suggests paymentClaimMethod optional
Frontend dashboard shows separate sections for wallets and fiat methods with no cross-referencing or completion percentage

gaps: No metrics on users with both wallet + fiat methods vs one or neither
Missing instrumentation for payment settlement method distribution (on-chain vs off-chain)
No tracking of payment claim validation failures (claims against non-existent fiat methods)
Missing metrics on primary method/wallet actual usage in job payments
No alerts for users with zero verified wallets and zero fiat methods (unpayable state)
Missing metrics on method type switching between jobs
No instrumentation for payment routing logic or fallback usage

businessValue.rationale: Determines payment options available to agents; affects job marketability. Both methods should be available but unclear strategy on prioritization. Strategic importance depends on target user geography.
businessValue.userImpact: Users need at least one payment method to be useful; offering both maximizes reach. Unverified wallets reduce agent confidence; fiat methods without crypto reduce flexibility.

features.name: Wallet Verification
features.description: Multi-method wallet ownership verification: signature-based (EVM), Privy identity token (embedded), or manual paste (unverified). On-chain transaction hash deduplication prevents double-spend.

issues: Nonce-based signature verification uses in-memory store; no persistence across server restarts (acceptable for single-server but breaks in multi-region)
Signature verification delegates to viem.verifyMessage but doesn't validate message format beyond presence check
Payment transaction deduplication uses paymentTxHash uniqueness constraint; collisions would surface as DB errors, not graceful rejection
Manual paste wallets (source='manual_paste') are unverified by default; could be abused for claiming others' addresses
Privy identity token fallback to REST API adds latency; no caching of fresh user data to avoid repeated calls
Cross-user wallet deletion prevented via humanId FK but relies on middleware auth; no explicit audit logging

existingMetrics: logger.warn for Privy wallet ownership verification failures
gaps: No metrics on verification method distribution (signature vs Privy vs manual paste)
Missing instrumentation for nonce generation rate and expiry timeouts
No alerts for repeated signature verification failures from same user
Missing tracking of paymentTxHash collision attempts (fraud indicator)
No metrics on manual_paste wallet adoption or subsequent verification conversion
Missing latency metrics for Privy identity token verification path

businessValue.rationale: Verification prevents fraudulent wallet claims and double-spend attacks. Direct security dependency for payment system; enables trust-based agent-human payments.
businessValue.userImpact: Verified wallets unlock agent trust; unverified manual-paste wallets may be flagged in payment flows. Affects which users can receive payments.

features.name: Affiliate / Referral
features.description: Single-tier referral program. Every human auto-enrolled as referrer. Affiliate record created on-demand. User B refers User A → referral recorded. User B completes profile → referral qualifies → credits awarded to User A. Bonus tiers: Tier1 (10 referrals = +50 credits), Tier2 (50 = +200), Tier3 (100 = +500). Anti-fraud: max 5 referrals per IP per day. Leaderboard shows top referrers (public, qualified signups only).

issues: REFERRAL_CONFIG hardcoded in affiliate.ts — no external config file
Milestone bonus awarded only when qualifiedSignups == threshold (not >=) — if user reaches 10 but had 5 earlier, no bonus
creditsRedeemed field on Affiliate not decremented anywhere visible — redemption logic missing
ipAddress and userAgent stored for fraud detection but no alerting system implemented
No spam detection (user B could refer 100 alt accounts)

gaps: No metrics on referral conversion funnel (signups → verified → completed profile)
Missing fraud detection alerting (same IP multiple signups)
No tracking of credit redemption rates or unused credits
Missing analytics on which referrers are most effective

businessValue.rationale: Referral program drives organic user acquisition and retention. Incentivizes existing users to recruit. Lower CAC than paid ads.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (incentive to grow network) — indirect revenue generator

features.name: Availability & Work Type
features.description: isAvailable boolean (open for work). workType: 'digital', 'physical', 'both'. workMode: 'REMOTE', 'ONSITE', 'HYBRID'. Capacity: weeklyCapacityHours (5, 10, 20, 30, 40+). Response time: 'within_1h', 'within_4h', 'within_24h', 'flexible'. Schedule pattern: 'morning', 'afternoon', 'evening', 'flexible'. Preferred task duration: 'micro' (1-2h), 'half_day' (3-5h), 'full_project' (10+h), 'any'. Earliest start date. All indexed for agent filtering.

issues: weeklyCapacityHours accepts 0-168 (0 = available but no capacity?) — semantic unclear
If all notification channels disabled, isAvailable forced to false (good safeguard but implicit logic)
earliestStartDate nullable but semantics unclear (null = immediately? or unknown?)
workType inferred from services but manually refinable — drift possible

existingMetrics: All fields indexed for agent search
gaps: No metrics on accuracy of capacity estimates (do humans stick to declared capacity?)
Missing tracking of response time compliance
No alerting on availability mismatches (marked available but no contact info)
Missing analytics on which capacity buckets convert best

businessValue.rationale: Availability signals are critical agent matching filters. Agents filter heavily by capacity, response time, work type. Reduces job proposal mismatches.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents (filtering relevance) and Humans (job fit)

features.name: Badge System
features.description: Dynamic SVG badges for embeddable widgets. Generic badge: 'Available on Human Pages'. Personalized badge: 'Hire {Name} on Human Pages' (if name fits). Cached 24h. No database writes — purely generative.

issues: SVG generation hardcodes Inter font URL from Google Fonts — potential latency if CDN slow
Width calculation (7px per char + 40px padding) is approximate and brittle for variable-width fonts
Error handling returns generic badge on any error (no logging of failures)

gaps: No metrics on badge clicks or usage
Missing analytics on which profiles embed badges
No tracking of badge render performance

businessValue.rationale: Nice-to-have embeddable widget for external promotion. Low direct impact on core job matching. Useful for viral growth but not critical.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (promotion tool) — optional feature

features.name: CV Upload & Parsing
features.description: Users upload PDF/DOCX CVs. Backend extracts text, parses with OpenAI to extract skills (explicit + inferred), education, certificates, languages, experience highlights, and years of experience. Bio is synthesized and sanitized. Files persisted to R2 with 5MB limit. Async processing with job-based polling.

issues: PDF extraction logic supports two versions of pdf-parse library (v1 and v2) with version detection — adds complexity
parseAndSaveCv duplicates extraction logic from POST route — could be refactored
No timeout handling for long-running OpenAI parses
skillSources JSON field lacks type safety (Record<string, string>)
Bio sanitization logic strips personal names but regex-based approach fragile for international names

gaps: No metrics on parse success/failure rates
No tracking of OpenAI API latency or token usage
No alerting on extraction failures (empty CVs, corrupted files)
Missing metrics for skill conflict detection (manual vs inferred)
No frontend error analytics for failed uploads

businessValue.rationale: CV parsing auto-populates critical searchable fields (skills, experience, education). Saves user 5-10 min vs manual entry. 80%+ of agents likely search by skill — directly impacts discovery.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (faster onboarding, richer profile) and Agents (better search results)

features.name: Education & Experience
features.description: Education entries: institution, country, degree, field, startYear, endYear. Certificates: name, issuer, year. Both sourced from CV or manual entry. Year field preserved for legacy compatibility. Ordered by endYear desc (most recent first).
backendRoutes: POST /api/cv/upload (creates education + certificates from CV parse)

issues: Legacy 'year' field on Education kept for backward compat but confusing (startYear/endYear preferred)
No validation on degree field (could be any string) — no enum for common degrees
Country field (2-char ISO or full name?) — unclear validation
Certificate.issuer optional but name required — could standardize (e.g., Coursera, AWS, etc.)
