No deduplication logic for education entries — manual uploads could create duplicates

gaps: No metrics on education/certificate frequency (what degrees, schools are most common)
Missing analytics on correlation between education and job success
No tracking of education entry sources (CV vs manual)
Missing alerting on suspicious educations (future dates, etc.)

businessValue.rationale: Education/certificates provide context for hiring decisions but aren't primary search filters like skills. Used in profile completeness scoring and trust signals.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents (resume signals) and Humans (profile richness)

features.name: Location & Timezone
features.description: User location: city-level display string (e.g., 'San Francisco, CA') + neighborhood (e.g., 'Mission District'). Lat/lng for radius-based search. locationGranularity: 'city' or 'neighborhood'. Timezone: IANA format (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'). Both indexed for fast agent filtering.

issues: Timezone validation accepts any string up to 100 chars — no validation against IANA database
Lat/lng validation range correct (-90/90, -180/180) but no bounds checking for impossible coordinates (e.g., lat > 85.051129 breaks Web Mercator)
locationGranularity only 'city' or 'neighborhood' — enum would be better than string
No geocoding fallback if lat/lng rejected by agent

existingMetrics: Indexed on [locationLat, locationLng] for radius search
Indexed on timezone for search filtering
lastActiveAt correlates with timezone for activity patterns
gaps: No metrics on timezone accuracy (how often mismatched with actual user location)
Missing analytics on radius search effectiveness
No alerting on invalid coordinates
Missing tracking of timezone change frequency

businessValue.rationale: Location and timezone are critical agent search filters. Agents filter by 'physical' work type + location or 'remote' + timezone. ~60% of jobs have location preferences.
businessValue.userImpact: Agents (filtering), Humans (geographic discoverability)

features.name: Profile Creation
features.description: Core profile identity fields (name, bio, photo). Users can create and update name, display name masking (FirstName L. or Anonymous), bio text with personal data sanitization, and manage privacy settings (hideRealName, hideContact).

issues: Name masking logic duplicated (publicHumanSelect and maskPublicName function)
Bio sanitization has regex-based approach that could be fragile for edge cases
Password hash exposed in query selection but properly stripped before response

gaps: No metrics for profile update failures
No tracking of which fields are most commonly updated
No performance metrics for large bio/name updates

businessValue.rationale: Profile creation is the foundational user identity feature; core to trust and searchability. Every human must have a profile to be discoverable.
businessValue.userImpact: All users (agents discover profiles; humans present identity)

features.name: Profile Photos & AI Photos
features.description: Upload profile photo (JPEG/PNG/WebP, 2MB max). Magic byte validation prevents spoofed files. Resized to 256x256, converted to WebP. Stored in R2. Moderation queue pending. Can import OAuth provider photos (Google, LinkedIn) — auto-approved. Can delete. Rate limit: 5 uploads/hour.
testFiles: src/tests/photos.test.ts (not in file list but referenced)

issues: OAuth photo URLs temporarily stored in oauthPhotoUrl — should be cleaned up aggressively (could be privacy leak)
Magic byte validation only checks first 12 bytes — theoretically bypassable with polyglot files
processProfileImage error handling is generic ('Invalid image file') — doesn't help debug Sharp processing failures
Moderation queue assumed to exist but not fully defined in this file

existingMetrics: profilePhotoStatus tracks state (none, pending, approved, rejected)
gaps: No metrics on moderation queue latency
Missing tracking of rejected photos (abuse patterns)
No alerting on image processing failures
Missing analytics on photo impact on job success rate

businessValue.rationale: Profile photo is critical trust signal. Visual identity increases job acceptance rates. Moderation reduces fraud/abuse.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (profile identity, trust) and Agents (screening, trust)

features.name: Schedule Management
features.description: Admin-only publication schedule (for internal staff content). Not a user-facing human profile field. Creates PublicationSchedule entries with platform (TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook, Blog), content type (video, article, short post, image post), scheduled/published times, status (draft, scheduled, publishing, published, failed, cancelled). Assigned to staff humans. Separate from human availability scheduling.

issues: No tests — critical gap for admin feature
platformMeta Json field lacks type safety
errorMessage field set on failure but no automatic retry logic
Assigned staff humans assumed to exist — no validation
Missing idempotency check for mark-published (could double-publish)

gaps: No metrics on publication success rates
Missing tracking of queue depth
No alerting on failed publications
Missing analytics on content performance by platform

businessValue.rationale: Admin-only content scheduling. Not directly tied to human profile or job matching. Internal operations tool.
businessValue.userImpact: None (humans) — staff internal tooling

features.name: Services Listing
features.description: Users create/update/delete service offerings (title, description, category, subcategory, price, currency, price unit). Services can be toggled active/inactive. Multiple services per user. Price unit normalized via UNIT_MAP (hourly → HOURLY, etc.).

issues: UNIT_MAP values are strings (not enums) — could be type-unsafe if frontend sends unexpected values
No validation on category/subcategory against allowed list
priceUnit mapping case-insensitive but not documented
Missing validation: priceMin requires priceUnit (schema validates, but error message could be clearer)

existingMetrics: Indexed on humanId for fast lookups
isActive flag for inactive service filtering
gaps: No metrics on service creation/update rates
No tracking of service popularity (how often searched, clicked, hired)
Missing insights on common service categories
No alerting on unusual pricing (outliers, potential spam)

businessValue.rationale: Services enable humans to market specific offerings. Agents filter by service category. Critical for matching but not as central as skills/bio.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (marketing offerings) and Agents (filtering by service type)

features.name: Skills Management
features.description: Users manage a flat array of skill tags (up to 50). Skills sourced from CV, inferred by OpenAI, or manually added. Skill normalization via synonym expansion (Hebrew → English, alternative terms → canonical). Denormalized skillSources JSON tracks origin (cv, cv_inferred, manual) for transparency.

issues: Synonym expansion hardcoded in humans.ts (SKILL_SYNONYMS and ENGLISH_SKILL_SYNONYMS dicts) — not maintainable long-term
Deduplication logic in zod transform duplicated (skills and equipment arrays)
No validation that skill tags are reasonable length or non-spam
skillSources JSON structure (Record<string, string>) lacks type safety
No audit log for skill changes — hard to debug user disputes

existingMetrics: Indexed on skills field for search performance
gaps: No metrics on skill frequency distribution
No alerting on unusual skill additions (potential spam/abuse)
No tracking of skill removal rates
Missing metrics on synonym expansion effectiveness

businessValue.rationale: Skills are primary agent search filter. 95%+ of agent job posts include requiredSkills filter. Poor skill management = poor discoverability.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (searchability) and Agents (search accuracy)

features.name: Vouch System
features.description: Peer-to-peer trust mechanism: Human A can vouch for Human B (max 10 given per person). Each vouch has optional text comment (200 char max). Vouchees receive 1 vouch point per vouch (denormalized in vouchCount). Unique constraint prevents duplicate vouches. Vouch can be revoked.

issues: Vouch limit (max 10 given) is hardcoded in route handler — no config file
Denormalized vouchCount could drift if deletes fail mid-transaction
No spam detection (same person can be vouched by many in short time)
Vouch comment sanitization missing (XSS risk if displayed in UI)

existingMetrics: vouchCount denormalized on Human for search ranking
gaps: No metrics on vouch creation rate or trends
No detection of vouch abuse patterns (same person being vouched 100x)
Missing analytics on vouch correlation with job success
No alerting on denormalized vouchCount mismatches

businessValue.rationale: Vouch system signals peer trust. Used in trust scoring and agent search ranking. Reduces fraud risk but not primary job matching driver.
businessValue.userImpact: Humans (trust signal) and Agents (sorting/filtering)
